2.note: Two olive trees and two lampstands before the Lord

9.note: Rejoice greatly O daughter of Zion! Shout O daughter of Jerusalem! Behold your king is coming to you; righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey on a colt the foal of a donkey — Matthew and John both cite Zechariah 9:9 as fulfilled in the triumphal entry; the King of peace entering on a donkey (not a war horse) is the messianic sign
Your king comes riding on a donkey
Your king comes riding on a donkey
Your king comes on a donkey — Palm Sunday entry in Luke

12.note: And they weighed out as my wages thirty pieces of silver — the shepherd's wages in Zechariah 11:12 are applied to Judas's price for betraying Jesus; Matthew explicitly cites this as fulfillment (using Jeremiah's name by prophetic attribution of the scripture) in Matt 27:9-10

13.note: Thirty pieces of silver thrown to the potter

10.note: They shall look on me whom they have pierced — Zechariah's oracle about the pierced one whom Jerusalem will mourn; John cites it at the crucifixion (they will look on the one they have pierced, John 19:37); Revelation applies it to the parousia (Rev 1:7: every eye will see him, even those who pierced him)
They will look on the one they pierced

7.note: Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered — Jesus quotes Zechariah 13:7 in Gethsemane as what will be fulfilled when he is arrested: strike the shepherd and the sheep of the flock will be scattered; the disciples' abandonment is the fulfillment of the Zechariah oracle about the smitten shepherd
Strike the shepherd and the sheep scatter
Strike the shepherd and the sheep scatter
